2. 三家分歧 — pi-astack 的选择
D1. 工具 schema 与返回值
D2. 与 vault_release 的合并范围
D12. Internal service vs LLM-facing tool

skipped.heading: Phase / 实施
skipped.reason: phase plans, file lists, LOC estimates -- logistics

skipped.heading: 5. 关键不变量汇总
skipped.reason: INV table restating captured mechanism sections

drafts.title: Transcript-based question UX causes sediment timing corruption
drafts.compiledTruth: LLM asking users questions via assistant text in the transcript causes sediment timing corruption: agent_end triggers before user answers, giving sediment a half-formed 'question without answer' context. This can cause misclassification of questions as facts or preferences, polluting the knowledge substrate. Additionally, plain markdown options lack structured UI affordances (chips, keyboard navigation, multi-select, masked input), making 3+ option comparison 2-5x slower than point-and-click.

drafts.title: Question is a yield point, requires dedicated tool substrate
drafts.compiledTruth: In an agent loop, 'waiting for user decision' is semantically equal to 'calling a tool' -- both are yield points requiring a dedicated substrate (pending state, cancel, timeout, redaction, structured UI), not a workaround through transcript text. This is the industry standard: Claude Code AskUserQuestion, Codex request_user_input, and OpenCode question all treat questions as first-class tools. pi-astack already proved this pattern with vault_release (grant state, UI authorization, audit lane, i18n); generalizing to prompt_user follows the same validated path.
drafts.sourceHeading: 1.2 三家方案为何成为行业标准

drafts.title: prompt_user and vault_release are separate LLM-facing tools
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user ('wait for user decision') and vault_release ('release sensitive data after authorization') must be separate LLM-facing tools even though they share the same PromptDialog UI substrate component. Merging them would let LLMs treat credential release as ordinary Q&A, weakening ADR 0014 invariant #5's namespace-level signal and polluting audit lanes with mixed semantics. The tool name itself carries semantic weight for prompt discipline: vault_release says 'this is a security boundary', prompt_user says 'this is a decision pause'.
drafts.sourceHeading: 2. 三家分歧 — pi-astack 的选择

drafts.title: type:secret returns placeholder only, raw value never enters LLM context
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user(type:'secret') provides masked input UX but returns only opaque placeholder [REDACTED_SECRET:<id>] to LLM context. Raw secret is held in PromptDialog component-local buffer and destroyed on dialog close. Audit records only lengthBucket ('1-8'|'9-32'|'>32'), never raw value, hash, prefix, suffix, or character count. type:'secret' cannot bypass vault_release; it is for one-time user-provided tokens consumed by extensions, not for releasing stored vault secrets.

drafts.title: Other free-text option forced-on, server-normalized, LLM cannot disable
drafts.compiledTruth: The 'Other' free-text escape hatch is always available in prompt_user dialogs and cannot be disabled by the LLM. Server-side normalization deduplicates synonymous labels to a canonical form. No forbidOther field exists in the schema. If a genuinely inescapable confirmation is needed (like Claude's ExitPlanMode), that must be a separate tool, not a parameter on prompt_user. The vault_release variant suppresses Other via caller-side configuration, which is unrelated to the LLM-facing schema.
drafts.sourceHeading: 2. 三家分歧 — pi-astack 的选择

drafts.title: Maximum 4 questions per prompt_user call
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user accepts 1-4 questions per call. Four is the user cognitive bandwidth ceiling validated by Claude Code's 12k user data. Three (Codex's limit) is too tight: forces a second tool call for one extra question, breaking atomic decision semantics. Unlimited (OpenCode's approach) lets LLMs batch unrelated questions together. LLMs should batch genuinely related decisions into a single call rather than chaining sequential prompt_user invocations back-to-back in the same turn.
drafts.sourceHeading: 2. 三家分歧 — pi-astack 的选择

drafts.title: Answers always string array; schema failures return reason not exception
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user schema has three key invariants: (1) answers[id] is always string[], even for single/text/secret types where length=1, matching industry consensus and eliminating LLM-side branching; (2) schema validation failures return {ok:false, reason:'schema-invalid'} rather than throwing exceptions, per ADR 0020 constraint 4 no-throw policy; (3) durationMs is returned in both success and failure branches for audit consistency. Empty text input is no-op on single but allowed as length-0 on multi. Secret type returns placeholder only in answer array.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. 工具 schema 与返回值

drafts.title: R7.2 removes all user-visible length limits; TUI auto-wraps
drafts.compiledTruth: All user-visible field length limits (header display cells, reason max length, question max length, option label words/length, payload byte cap) are removed. TUI text components auto-wrap via wrapToWidth (CJK-aware). LLM self-decides label length. PromptUserOption merges label+description into a single 'label' field; old LLMs passing 'description' get it silent-dropped by validator (non-breaking migration). The only remaining checks are structural+safety: 1-4 questions, 2-4 options, ID_REGEX, VALID_TYPES, field rejections, control chars rejection, timeoutSec clamp.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. 工具 schema 与返回值

drafts.title: vault_release retains independent tool identity with shared PromptDialog UI
drafts.compiledTruth: vault_release stays as a separate LLM-facing tool (not merged into prompt_user) because: (a) it has gone through multi-round audits on sub-pi guards and vault identity edges via ADR 0014/0019; (b) prompt guidelines differ fundamentally -- grant authorization vs asking a decision; (c) grant state has session/remember models while prompt_user has no stable join key (questions are dynamic, not keyed). Both use the same PromptDialog UI component with different variants. New audit lane 'prompt_user' is added; vault_release and prompt_user audit lanes stay separate with different schema (release/release_denied vs ask/response).
drafts.sourceHeading: D2. 与 vault_release 的合并范围

drafts.title: Separate audit lanes for prompt_user and vault_release
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user gets its own audit lane separate from vault_release (lane:'vault_release') and bash_inject (lane:'bash_output'). They share the low-level safeAuditAppend helper but have different retrieval semantics: vault auditors query by key ('which secrets were released'), prompt_user auditors query by turn ('when did LLM stop and ask what'). Merging lanes would force consumers to switch on op type continuously. Schema also differs: vault op enum is release/release_denied/release_blocked; prompt_user is ask/response/blocked.
drafts.sourceHeading: D2. 与 vault_release 的合并范围

drafts.title: Three-layer sub-pi guard fully inherited via PI_ABRAIN_DISABLED
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user inherits the existing three-layer sub-pi guard: Layer 1 (dispatch spawn injects PI_ABRAIN_DISABLED=1 in child env, positioned after ...process.env to prevent user export bypass); Layer 2 (abrain activate() early-returns, tool not registered in sub-pi registry); Layer 3 (handler fail-fast checks env var + ctx.hasUI before any UI call). No new env var needed. ctx.ui.custom missing is NOT a first-line reject -- it falls through to chained select/input fallback path instead. Secret type on fallback returns ui-unavailable (ctx.ui.input lacks masked mode).

drafts.title: Four cancellation triggers drain all pending prompts with inner controller isolation
drafts.compiledTruth: Pending prompt_user calls are drained to finite resolution by four triggers: user Cancel/Esc/'No' button (user-rejected), ctx.signal abort on turn cancel (cancelled), session_shutdown event (cancelled), and timeoutSec expiry (timeout). Each prompt holds its own inner AbortController that listens to outer ctx.signal but does NOT forward abort back to sibling tool calls -- preventing one cancelled prompt from killing other in-flight tools in the same turn. cancelAllPending drains the pending Map on session_shutdown and resolves all with durationMs.

drafts.title: Timeout clamped to [30, 1800] seconds; no infinite wait
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user timeoutSec is clamped to minimum 30s (under 30s causes LLM urgency bias and bad UX by setting 10s deadlines) and maximum 1800s/30min (hard cap prevents malicious/incompetent parameters; beyond 30min user has likely left). timeoutSec=0 is explicitly rejected -- it would mean infinite pending, violating fail-closed. Default is 600s/10min. Last 30 seconds show countdown via DynamicBorder timing API rather than custom setInterval to avoid full-screen repaint jitter. vault_release timeout harmonization is deferred to follow-up.

drafts.title: Secret redaction at every write endpoint with independent defense layers
drafts.compiledTruth: type:'secret' raw values are blocked at every write endpoint: tool result returns [REDACTED_SECRET:<id>] placeholder only; audit stores only lengthBucket without raw value, hash, prefix, suffix, or character count; sediment transcript pre-pass redacts secret placeholders as defense-in-depth; RPC log and session jsonl redact before disk write. All 4 user-visible fields (reason, header, question, option.label) go through redactCredentials+sanitizeForMemory at both handler entry AND audit write -- independent two-site redaction ensures no single omission leaks credentials (e.g. URL:password in reason field).

drafts.title: redactCredentials elevated to shared redact.ts primitive within abrain
drafts.compiledTruth: The redactCredentials() function previously local to git-sync.ts is elevated to extensions/abrain/redact.ts as a shared primitive, joined by new helpers: redactSecretAnswer (whole-value placeholder), lengthBucket (bucket-only, no char count), and redactPromptParams (applies redaction to all user-visible prompt fields). git-sync.ts re-exports from './redact' maintaining backward-compatible import paths. The primitive stays inside abrain (not _shared/) -- promotion to cross-extension shared API requires a separate security review once a second real consumer appears.

drafts.title: Inline editor replacement is the primary prompt_user UI path
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user primary UI path uses ctx.ui.custom() with overlay:false -- inline editor region replacement, not a centered popup. This matches Claude Code/Codex/OpenCode industry standard and pi's built-in ui.select/input/confirm/editor components (all bottom-inline, not overlay). Original overlay assumption in R1-R5 was a misreading of pi SDK. Terminal width rejection (<60 cols) is deleted: inline Text component auto-wraps, supporting 40+ col split-pane tmux, SSH mobile, and iPad mini. Custom() unavailable falls through to chained select/input fallback; secret type is ui-unavailable on fallback (no masked input).

drafts.title: Prompt-first abuse governance with single concurrent hard gate
drafts.compiledTruth: LLM abuse of prompt_user is governed by three layers: (1) promptGuidelines instructing batching, avoiding confirmation, checking memory_search first; (2) audit visibility with both prompt_request and prompt_response rows written per call for sediment analysis; (3) a single hard gate: at most 1 concurrent pending prompt_user per session, with second concurrent call returning schema-invalid with distinct detail ('another prompt is pending') -- not reused 'cancelled' reason to avoid ambiguity. A soft cap warns via detail field after 2+ calls per turn but does not reject. Sediment treats prompt_user answers as high-trust user-attested evidence in curator prompt.

drafts.title: Compaction-tuner defers compaction when user-facing overlays are pending
drafts.compiledTruth: compaction-tuner skips agent_end compaction when any user-facing overlay has pending state, checking two independent hooks: getPendingPromptCount()>0 for prompt_user and isVaultDialogInFlight() for vault authorization dialogs. Each produces a distinct audit reason (prompt_user_pending vs vault_dialog_pending) for debugging. Both hooks are hardened via Object.defineProperty configurable:false writable:false to prevent silent unbinding. Deferred compaction retries on next agent_end. The two substrates are checked separately (not a unified 'any overlay' hook) because state semantics differ (count vs boolean) and future evolution may diverge.

drafts.title: Internal askPromptUser service separated from LLM-facing tool
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user is an LLM-facing tool; slash commands like /about-me call the internal askPromptUser(ctx, params, options?) service instead, with caller-specified audit lane. This prevents: (a) audit lane pollution (slash operations appearing in LLM tool lane); (b) LLMs from seeing slash-generated fence construction paths; (c) trust double-counting -- /about-me answers wrapped in MEMORY-ABOUT-ME fence carry Lane G fence trust, not toolResult:prompt_user trust. The service uses the same schema validation but skips sub-pi guard (slash only fires in main session, where user is present). The service is the single internal entry point for any non-LLM caller needing structured user input.
drafts.sourceHeading: D12. Internal service vs LLM-facing tool

drafts.title: P0 secret type ships schema/UI/redaction only, no callback API
drafts.compiledTruth: P0 scope for type:'secret' is deliberately limited to schema declaration, masked UI rendering, and full redaction pipeline verification via smoke tests. No caller-side callback API (onSecretAnswered) is exposed. Raw secret has zero code-consumable paths in P0 -- it exists only in PromptDialog component buffer and is destroyed on dialog close. This prevents shipping an un-reviewed API surface. Full secret consumption requires a follow-up ADR. P0 value is UX validation, redaction boundary verification before real traffic, and deferred API design.

drafts.title: vault_release UI migration to PromptDialog with fallback retention period
drafts.compiledTruth: When vault_release's UI path migrates from ui.select to ctx.ui.custom(PromptDialog, {variant:'vault_release'}), the original ui.select path is retained as fallback (not removed). The fallback stays through: (a) two release cycles; (b) smoke-abrain-vault-reader covering both PromptDialog and fallback paths with at least 28 assertions; (c) real user scenario showing at least 1 week without regression. Only then is the fallback removed in a separate PR. This prevents untested UI migration from breaking the only authorized secret-release path.
drafts.sourceHeading: D2. 与 vault_release 的合并范围
